flashcards.front: Define ISO 25010 quality model and name its two main quality divisions.
flashcards.back: ISO 25010 is an international standard defining software quality characteristics. It divides quality into two parts: internal/external quality (operational attributes) and quality in use (user-specific attributes measured in real environments).

flashcards.front: What is LOC (Lines of Code) metric and what are its two variants?
flashcards.back: LOC measures code size by counting executable statements. KLOC (thousand lines of code) is used for larger systems. Halstead's LOC counts operators and operands, while physical LOC counts all lines including comments and blank lines.

flashcards.front: Define Coupling in software engineering and list its types from highest to lowest dependency.
flashcards.back: Coupling measures interdependency between modules. Types (highest to lowest): Content Coupling > Common Coupling > External Coupling > Control Coupling > Stamp Coupling > Data Coupling. Low coupling indicates maintainable, reusable code.

flashcards.front: What is Cohesion and list the seven types from lowest to highest.
flashcards.back: Cohesion measures how strongly related a module's responsibilities are. Types (lowest to highest): Coincidental < Logical < Temporal < Procedural < Communicational < Sequential < Functional. High cohesion (functional) is desirable.

flashcards.front: Define DIT (Depth of Inheritance Tree) metric.
flashcards.back: DIT measures the maximum inheritance depth from root class to leaf class in class hierarchy. Greater DIT implies more complexity but promotes code reuse. Maximum DIT should typically not exceed 5-7 for maintainability.

flashcards.front: Define RFC (Response for a Class) and its formula.
flashcards.back: RFC measures the total number of methods that can be executed in response to a message received by an object. Formula: RFC = |RS| (size of response set), where response set includes all methods callable by the class. Higher RFC indicates greater complexity.

flashcards.front: Compare McCall's Quality Model with ISO 25010.
flashcards.back: McCall's model (1977) has 11 quality factors in 3 categories (product revision, product operation, product transition). ISO 25010 (2011) has 8 characteristics in 2 divisions (internal/external quality, quality in use). ISO 25010 is more comprehensive and modern.

flashcards.front: A class has 15 methods. During testing, 12 methods can be invoked directly. Calculate RFC.
flashcards.back: RFC = Number of methods in response set = 12. Since RFC includes all methods that can be invoked in response to any message, the value is 12. High RFC (>50) indicates complex class requiring decomposition.
